questions.q: The concept of 'Basic Structure' of the Constitution was introduced in the year:
What is the term given to the power given to the President to promulgate ordinances in cases of emergency?
Which of the following is not a part of the Directive Principles of State Policy?
Explain the concept of 'Judicial Review' in the context of the Indian Constitution. (10 marks)
questions.answer: The Indian Constitution grants the power of judicial review to the Supreme Court, which enables it to examine the constitutionality of laws and government actions. This power is essential for ensuring that the government does not overstep its authority and that the rights of citizens are protected.

questions.q: Who among the following is the chief adviser to the President on matters relating to the appointment of the judges of the Supreme Court and the High Courts?
Describe the functions of the Election Commission of India. (5 marks)
questions.answer: The Election Commission of India is responsible for conducting free and fair elections to the Parliament and the State Legislatures. It ensures the registration of voters, the setting up of polling stations, and the conduct of elections in accordance with the provisions of the Representation of the People Act.

questions.q: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to freedom of speech and expression?
What is the term given to the process of amendment of the Constitution through a two-thirds majority in both Houses of Parliament?
List any three Fundamental Rights that are not justiciable. (3 marks)
questions.answer: Right to Constitutional Remedies, Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression, and Right to Freedom of Assembly

questions.q: The 'Basic Structure' of the Constitution was first enunciated in the case of:
questions.answer: Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
